steps.title: Interpret the function structure
steps.content:
The function $f(m) = 30 - 3m$ represents the remaining balance after renting $m$ movies.

This follows the general pattern:
remaining balance = initial amount − (cost per movie) × m
Here, the \$30 is the initial amount, and the coefficient of $m$ tells us how the balance changes per movie.

steps.title: Determine the cost per movie
steps.content:
In $f(m) = 30 - 3m$, the coefficient of $m$ is $-3$. The negative value indicates the balance decreases; the magnitude tells us the decrease per movie.

The balance decreases by \$3 for each movie rented, so each rental costs \$3. That matches choice B — B is correct.
